acceptance.claim: The user opens settings, starts GitHub browser authorization, returns through the OAuth callback, and sees GitHub authorization configured on the sync screen.
The OAuth redirect repository builds a GitHub authorize URL with client_id, redirect_uri, scope, state, code_challenge, and code_challenge_method=S256.
The OAuth redirect repository validates callback state, maps callback errors, and exchanges code with client_id, redirect_uri, and code_verifier without requiring a client secret.
The token settings BLoC starts browser authorization, handles callback success/failure, stores the returned token securely, and reports missing configuration failures.
AppConfig exposes OAuth redirect configuration and dependency injection uses the deterministic fixture in dev and the GitHub API-backed redirect repository outside dev.
